purpose: Four Claude Code hook scripts generated by ultrainit for the open-webui project. They enforce code quality and safety automatically during Claude agent sessions: auto-formatting Python and frontend files after edits, blocking writes to protected files (credentials, migration files, secrets), and reminding developers to update i18n locale files when Svelte/TS files with translation calls are modified.

architecture.overview:
All four hooks share an identical structural skeleton: bash strict mode, stdin-based JSON input, python3 inline JSON extraction, path-scoped filtering, and tool invocation or exit-code signaling. The hooks are purely event-driven — no shared state, no inter-hook communication, no config files. Each hook is self-contained and idempotent. The design philosophy is 'narrow fast, do little': every hook short-circuits at the earliest possible condition check and uses `|| true` or `exit 0` to stay non-fatal for non-applicable inputs.

The hook set covers three event lifecycle slots in settings.json:
- PreToolUse (guard-protected-files.sh, on Edit)
- PostToolUse (autoformat-python.sh on Edit, autoformat-frontend.sh on Write)
- Stop (i18n-reminder.sh, on session end)

Each hook is a standalone executable bash script (~40–80 lines), all made executable (`chmod +x`). There are no shared libraries, no sourced helper files, no environment variable contracts between hooks.

subdirectories.purpose: Flat directory of executable bash hook scripts. No subdirectory organization — all four scripts live at the same level.

architecture.data_flow: Claude Code calls each hook by piping a JSON event payload to its stdin. The hook reads all of stdin into `$input`, checks for empty (exit 0 if empty — handles no-op calls gracefully), then uses an inline python3 snippet to extract `data['tool_input']['file_path']` from the JSON. After extraction, the hook applies a series of bash `[[ ... =~ pattern ]]` regex guards to narrow scope: first by file extension, then by directory prefix, then by tool availability (`command -v`), then by file existence (`[[ -f "$file_path" ]]`). Only after passing all guards does the hook execute its action (run formatter, emit to stderr with exit 2, or run git diff). Output to stderr is visible in the Claude session; exit 2 blocks the tool call (PreToolUse only); exit 0 is always non-blocking for PostToolUse and Stop hooks.

key_files.purpose: PreToolUse hook on Edit events. Hard-blocks (exit 2) writes to .env files, .webui_secret_key, existing Alembic migrations, and existing Peewee migrations. Soft-warns (stderr only, no exit) on security-critical Python auth files and the RBAC permissions TypeScript file. Each block includes a human-readable reason and a safe alternative workflow.

PostToolUse hook on Write events. Runs `npx prettier --write` on any .ts/.svelte/.js/.css/.json file written inside src/ or cypress/ directories (but not /backend/ or node_modules). Uses `--plugin-search-dir .` to scope Prettier plugin discovery.
PostToolUse hook on Edit events. Runs `ruff format` on any .py file inside /backend/ or open_webui directories. Silently skips if ruff is not on PATH.

Stop hook (session end). Checks git diff (unstaged and staged) for modified .svelte/.ts files in src/ that contain `$i18n.t(` calls. If found, emits a reminder to run `npm run i18n:parse` before pushing. Does not block — informational only.
Wires all four hooks to their lifecycle events: guard on PreToolUse/Edit, autoformat-python on PostToolUse/Edit, autoformat-frontend on PostToolUse/Write, i18n-reminder on Stop.

patterns.name: Stdin JSON extraction via inline python3
patterns.description:
Every hook reads the Claude Code event payload from stdin using `input=$(cat)`, then pipes it through a python3 one-liner to extract `tool_input.file_path`. The python3 snippet uses try/except to return an empty string on any error — never fatal. The outer bash then uses `2>/dev/null || echo ""` as a second safety layer. This pattern avoids requiring `jq` in hooks (unlike the orchestrator) and handles malformed JSON gracefully.

Canonical form seen in all three file-aware hooks:
```bash
file_path=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
import json, sys
try:
    data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    path = data.get('tool_input', {}).get('file_path', '')
    print(path)
except Exception:
    pass
" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
```

patterns.name: Funnel-narrowing guard chain
patterns.description: Each hook applies multiple sequential `[[ ... ]] && exit 0` guards before executing its main action. The order is always: (1) empty input guard, (2) file path extraction + empty guard, (3) extension filter, (4) directory scope filter, (5) tool availability check, (6) file existence check. Each guard exits cleanly — never with an error — so failed extraction or out-of-scope files are silently ignored. This makes hooks composable and safe to apply broadly without false positives.

patterns.name: Tiered exit code signaling (block vs. warn)
patterns.description: guard-protected-files.sh uses two distinct response levels. Hard blocks use `exit 2` after printing to stderr with BLOCKED:/Reason:/Safe alternative: labels — this causes Claude Code to abort the Edit tool call entirely. Soft warnings use only `echo ... >&2` with no exit override (falls through to `exit 0`) — the Edit proceeds but the developer sees the warning. The choice of which tier to use maps directly to the risk level documented in CLAUDE.md's Security-Critical Areas section.

patterns.name: Git-diff-based session-end reminder
patterns.description: i18n-reminder.sh is architecturally different from the other three: it doesn't parse `tool_input.file_path` from stdin at all. Instead, it runs `git diff --name-only` and `git diff --cached --name-only` to find all modified .svelte/.ts files in src/, then greps each for `$i18n.t(` patterns. This works on the accumulated session state rather than per-tool events. The reminder message references the exact CI workflow file (`.github/workflows/format-build-frontend.yaml`) to explain the failure mode.

patterns.name: Tool availability soft-guard
patterns.description: Both autoformat hooks check `command -v npx` / `command -v ruff` before executing the formatter. If the tool is not installed, the hook exits cleanly with `exit 0` rather than failing with a missing command error. This allows hooks to work across environments (CI containers, developer machines with different tool installations) without crashing the agent session.

conventions.naming: All scripts are kebab-case with .sh extension: `autoformat-frontend.sh`, `autoformat-python.sh`, `guard-protected-files.sh`, `i18n-reminder.sh`. Names encode action + scope (autoformat-{lang}) or intent + subject (guard-{domain}, {topic}-reminder). All files are executable (`chmod +x`).
conventions.imports: No sourcing of shared libraries. Each hook is fully self-contained. python3, git, npx, ruff are invoked via PATH — no absolute paths hardcoded. The settings.json references hooks by relative path from the project root: `.claude/hooks/hookname.sh`.
conventions.error_handling: All hooks start with `set -euo pipefail` for strict mode. Formatter invocations append `|| true` to prevent formatting errors from propagating as hook failures (e.g., `npx prettier ... 2>/dev/null || true`, `ruff format ... 2>/dev/null || true`). Python extraction uses try/except catch-all. git commands append `|| true` in i18n-reminder.sh. The only intentional non-zero exit is `exit 2` in guard-protected-files.sh for hard blocks.
other: stderr is used for all user-visible output (BLOCKED:, WARNING:, REMINDER: messages). stdout is not used for communication — only the exit code signals block vs. allow.
All blocking messages follow a 3-line format: BLOCKED/WARNING line, Reason: line, Safe alternative: line. This mirrors the CLAUDE.md prohibition pattern (every 'don't' has a 'do instead').
Empty input guard (`if [[ -z "$input" ]]; then exit 0; fi`) appears identically in all four hooks as the very first check after stdin read.
Regex patterns use bash `=~` operator with ERE syntax. Migration file patterns use character classes: `[a-f0-9]+_` for Alembic hex IDs, `[0-9]+_` for Peewee numeric IDs.
The Alembic migration guard checks `[[ -f "$file_path" ]]` before blocking — new migration files (not yet on disk) are allowed through. Only editing existing migration files is blocked.

internal: test-repos/CLAUDE.md (security section documents the rationale for guard-protected-files patterns)

external_key.name: python3
external_key.purpose: Inline JSON extraction from Claude Code's stdin event payload in all three file-aware hooks

external_key.name: npx (Node.js)
external_key.purpose: Runs prettier for frontend autoformatting in autoformat-frontend.sh

external_key.name: prettier
external_key.purpose: Frontend formatter invoked via npx with --plugin-search-dir . flag

external_key.name: ruff
external_key.purpose: Python formatter invoked directly in autoformat-python.sh

external_key.name: git
external_key.purpose: Used in i18n-reminder.sh to detect modified Svelte/TS files via git diff

external_key.name: bash (>=4.0)
external_key.purpose: All hooks use bash-specific features: [[ ]], =~ regex, set -euo pipefail

domain_terms.meaning: Claude Code hook event that fires before a tool call executes. exit 2 from this hook cancels the tool call entirely.
Claude Code hook event that fires after a tool call completes. Exit code does not cancel anything — used for side effects like autoformatting.
Claude Code hook event that fires when the agent session ends. Used by i18n-reminder.sh for end-of-session reminders.
The Claude Code convention for blocking hooks: exiting with code 2 from a PreToolUse hook cancels the pending tool call and shows stderr to the user.
The JSON field in Claude Code's hook stdin payload that contains the file path being written/edited by the triggering tool call.
The settings.json field that scopes a hook to a specific tool name (e.g., 'Edit', 'Write'). Hooks without a matcher fire on all tool calls of that event type.
Current database migration system in open-webui; migration files live in backend/open_webui/migrations/versions/ with hex-prefixed filenames. Existing ones must never be edited.
Legacy migration system (001-018) in backend/open_webui/internal/migrations/. Frozen — new migrations must use Alembic only.
Svelte reactive i18n translation call. Adding new calls requires running `npm run i18n:parse` to update locale JSON files or CI format gate fails.

gotchas.issue: autoformat-python.sh fires on Edit only; autoformat-frontend.sh fires on Write only. This asymmetry means: writing a new Python file (Write event) does not trigger ruff, and editing an existing frontend file (Edit event) does not trigger prettier.
gotchas.solution: If full coverage is needed, both hooks should be registered for both Edit and Write events in settings.json. The current setup is intentional (Edit = modify existing, Write = create new) but creates a coverage gap for Python file creation and frontend file edits.

gotchas.issue: guard-protected-files.sh is registered only on Edit events, not Write. An agent could create a new .env file via Write — the guard would not fire.
gotchas.solution: Add guard-protected-files.sh as a PreToolUse hook for Write events in settings.json as well. The script's logic already handles file creation gracefully (the Alembic check uses `[[ -f "$file_path" ]]` before blocking — it would need adjustment for .env creation).

gotchas.issue: i18n-reminder.sh uses unquoted `for f in $modified_svelte` which is subject to word splitting on filenames with spaces.
gotchas.solution: Use `while IFS= read -r f; do ... done <<< "$modified_svelte"` for robust filename handling. Unlikely to matter in practice (source file paths rarely have spaces) but technically unsafe.

gotchas.issue: The python3 stdin extraction in the three file-aware hooks reads from a pipe (`echo "$input" | python3 -c ...`), which means it reads the JSON string representation of `$input`, not a file. If `$input` contains unescaped characters that bash interprets specially in double-quoted strings, extraction could silently fail and return empty string.
gotchas.solution: The `2>/dev/null || echo ""` fallback ensures the hook exits cleanly rather than crashing. In practice Claude Code always emits well-formed JSON, so this is defensive programming that works.

gotchas.issue: autoformat-frontend.sh passes `--plugin-search-dir .` to prettier, which limits plugin discovery to the project root. If prettier plugins are installed in a non-standard location or a monorepo subpackage, they won't be found.
gotchas.solution: This is intentional — scopes Prettier to the project's own plugin set and prevents accidental use of globally-installed plugins. If plugins are needed from a subdir, update the flag.

gotchas.issue: i18n-reminder.sh reads stdin (the Stop event payload) but never uses its contents — it only calls git diff. The stdin read is present only for structural consistency with other hooks and the empty-input guard pattern.
gotchas.solution: No action needed. The Stop event payload may not contain file path information anyway — the git diff approach is the correct pattern for session-end checks.

gotchas.issue: The guard for `.env` files uses the regex `\.env($|\.)` to match `.env` and `.env.something` but explicitly excludes `.env.example`. A file named `.environment` would NOT match (correct). A file named `.env.production` WOULD be blocked (intended but worth knowing).
gotchas.solution: The regex is correctly scoped. Be aware that ALL `.env.*` variants except `.env.example` are blocked — including `.env.local`, `.env.staging`, `.env.production`. This is intentional per CLAUDE.md.

skill_opportunities.name: add-claude-hook
skill_opportunities.description: Guide adding a new hook script to .claude/hooks/ for this project — picking the right lifecycle event, writing the stdin extraction boilerplate, choosing exit 2 vs warning pattern, and wiring into settings.json.
workflow_steps: Determine lifecycle event: PreToolUse (blocking), PostToolUse (side-effect), or Stop (reminder)
Copy the stdin extraction + empty-guard boilerplate from an existing hook
Add domain-specific filtering (extension, path prefix, tool availability)
Decide block (exit 2 + BLOCKED/Reason/Safe alternative message) or warn (stderr only) or act (run tool)
Make script executable and add entry to .claude/settings.json under the appropriate event/matcher

skill_opportunities.name: audit-hook-coverage
skill_opportunities.description: Audit whether the current four hooks have coverage gaps — particularly the Edit/Write asymmetry for formatters and the missing Write guard for protected files.
workflow_steps: Read all hooks and map their event bindings from settings.json
Cross-reference with CLAUDE.md Security-Critical Areas and tooling sections
Identify files that can bypass hooks (Write vs Edit gaps, missing PreToolUse on Write)
Propose additions or matcher changes to close gaps

commands.purpose: Test the guard hook manually with a simulated Edit payload
Test frontend autoformat hook with a Svelte file path
Trigger i18n reminder check against current git diff state
